body:
## Summary
- Post a structured terminal stop comment when a resumed parent meta-orchestrator stops without dispatching another action.
- Dedupe exact trusted stop comments using the existing orchestrate stop marker.
- Document the parent terminal stop behavior.

## Tests
- `npm run build`
- `env -u BASE_BRANCH node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js`
- `git diff --check`

Closes #95

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

implementation succeeded; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777774946188 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNjc1NDM2ODY6aW1wbGVtZW50Ojk1OnJldmlldzoxMTI6Mw -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | pass | 6/6 | PR adds `commentOnTerminalMetaOrchestratorStop` for resumed parent `orchestrate` stops, posting a visible final comment when no follow-up workflow is dispatched. |
| coding_workflow / specific | Default follow-up orchestration to one-shot | pass | 8/8 | The new terminal parent stop path explicitly avoids dispatching another workflow; tests assert no `actions/workflows/` dispatch and `dispatchPayload` is `null`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR follows the accepted #95 plan and parent #104 child sequence after #90; the implementation matches the requested final parent stop UX. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is limited to `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts`, focused handoff tests, and the orchestrator technical doc. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Reuses existing stop comment creation, `fetchIssueComments`, `createIssueComment`, `isTrustedActorLogin`, `normalizeToken`, and hidden marker conventions instead of adding a parallel mechanism. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md` now documents parent terminal stop comments and trusted exact duplicate skipping. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Dedupe lookup failures are surfaced with `console.warn`, and the code falls back to posting the stop comment rather than silently treating the state as clean. |

## Notes

- The strongest rubric fit is the workflow-stop UX: PR #112 directly addresses #95 by making resumed parent meta-orchestrator stops visible instead of silent.
- The implementation is narrow and consistent with existing orchestration state markers.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric violations found. The PR satisfies the applicable selected rubrics with focused code, regression coverage, and matching docs.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #112 addresses issue #95, where resumed parent `orchestrate` runs could stop silently after child work completed.
- It adds a visible terminal stop comment for parent agent-mode issue orchestration when no follow-up action is dispatched.
- The change reuses existing stop-comment machinery, adds trusted exact-match dedupe, and updates orchestrator docs.
- This is part of the broader #104 stacked orchestrator-fixes sequence, following #90/#108 comment-format work.

## Review
The reviewers agree there are no blocking correctness issues and that the PR solves the intended UX gap surgically. One concrete test-coverage gap remains around the new guard scope; I posted an inline comment for that.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Missing negative guard coverage | WARNING | The new terminal parent stop behavior is covered for the agent/issue happy path, but not for `heuristics` mode or `pull_request` targets. Focused tests would lock down the intended scope. |
| Exact-match dedupe scope is narrow | INFO | One reviewer noted that dedupe only skips exact trusted duplicate bodies. That matches the PR’s stated “exact duplicate” behavior, but broader cross-body/rerun dedupe would require an intentional design choice. |

## Progress
- Codex review found no findings and validated `npm run build`, the focused compiled handoff test, and `git diff --check`.
- Rubrics review passed 100/100 across the applicable workflow and coding rubrics.
- Existing PR conversation already acknowledges the #95 UX gap and the #104 stacked child plan.
- No prior inline comments existed; one new inline comment was posted for the negative guard coverage gap.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Missing negative guard coverage</summary>

**Cause:** `commentOnTerminalMetaOrchestratorStop` has new guard logic limiting comments to agent-mode issue orchestration, but tests only cover the posting path and exact-match dedupe path.

**Candidate solutions:** Add focused tests asserting no orchestrate-stop comment is posted when `AUTOMATION_MODE` is `heuristics`, and when `TARGET_KIND` / `sourceTargetKind` is `pull_request`.

**Comments:** This is not a functional blocker, but it is a small, safe regression-coverage fix for the main scope constraint.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: The remaining branch work is concrete, low-risk test coverage and is safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Add negative-path tests for `heuristics` mode and `pull_request` targets so terminal parent stop comments remain scoped to agent-mode issue orchestration.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 4/10).

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | pass | 6/6 | PR adds a terminal parent orchestrator stop comment path for resumed agent-mode issue orchestration when no follow-up dispatch occurs. |
| coding_workflow / specific | Default follow-up orchestration to one-shot | pass | 8/8 | Tests assert terminal parent stops do not dispatch `actions/workflows/` and leave `dispatchPayload` as `null`; fix-pr added negative coverage for heuristics and PR targets. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | Implementation matches issue #95’s approved plan and the #104 stacked sequence after #90 completed with SHIP. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is limited to `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts`, focused handoff tests, and `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Reuses existing stop-comment formatting path, `fetchIssueComments`, `createIssueComment`, `isTrustedActorLogin`, `normalizeToken`, and hidden marker conventions. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Orchestrator technical docs now describe terminal parent stop comments and trusted exact duplicate skipping. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Existing stop-comment dedupe lookup failures are surfaced with `console.warn`, and the path falls back to posting rather than silently treating failed inspection as clean state. |

## Notes

- The strongest rubric fit is the workflow-stop UX: PR #112 directly fixes #95 by making resumed parent meta-orchestrator stops visible.
- The post-review fix addressed the main coverage gap by adding negative tests for `heuristics` mode and `pull_request` targets.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric violations found. The PR satisfies the applicable selected rubrics with focused code, regression coverage, and matching documentation.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #112 fixes issue #95 by making resumed parent `orchestrate` runs post a visible terminal stop comment when no follow-up action is dispatched.
- The implementation is part of the broader #104 stacked orchestrator-fixes plan, following the earlier child work on orchestrator comment formatting.
- It reuses the existing orchestrate stop-comment path, adds trusted exact-match dedupe, and scopes the new terminal comment behavior to agent-mode issue orchestration.
- The PR also updates focused handoff tests and the orchestrator technical documentation.

## Review
The reviewers agree the PR is now complete and safe to ship. The earlier coverage warning was addressed by the fix-pr pass, and no unresolved blocking, warning, or required branch-change issues remain.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues remain | INFO | Codex, Claude, and the rubrics review found no unresolved required changes after the follow-up fix added the missing negative-path coverage. |

## Progress
- The prior inline warning about missing negative guard coverage was addressed with tests for `heuristics` mode and `pull_request` targets.
- Reviewer validation passed: `npm run build`, focused `orchestrate-handoff-cli` tests, full `npm test`, and `git diff --check`.
- Rubrics review passed 100/100, including surgical scope, docs sync, one-shot follow-up behavior, and visible workflow-stop reporting.
- Informational notes about exact-match dedupe, the structured initial-stop format, and a low-risk round-1 edge case were treated as non-blocking and not automation action items.

## Recommended Next Step
`NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION`: no unresolved actionable branch work remains, so the PR is ready for human merge review.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

title: Post parent orchestrator terminal stop comments
